original_theorem:
If $\mathbf{X}$ and $\mathbf{Y}$ are
 in $\R^n,$ then
$$
|\mathbf{X}-\mathbf{Y}|\ge\left| |\mathbf{X}|-|\mathbf{Y}|\right|.
$$

We can assume the following statement(s) without a proof. 

If $\mathbf{X}$ and $\mathbf{Y}$ are in $\R^n,$ then
\begin{equation}\label{eq:5.1.6}
|\mathbf{X}+\mathbf{Y}|\le |\mathbf{X}|+|\mathbf{Y}|,
\end{equation}
with equality if and only if one of the vectors is a nonnegative
multiple of the other$.$
original_proof:
Since
$$
\mathbf{X}=\mathbf{Y}+(\mathbf{X}-\mathbf{Y}),
$$
Theorem~\ref{thmtype:5.1.6} implies that
$$
|\mathbf{X}|\le |\mathbf{Y}|+|\mathbf{X}-\mathbf{Y}|,
$$
which is equivalent to
$$
|\mathbf{X}|-|\mathbf{Y}|\le |\mathbf{X}-\mathbf{Y}|.
$$
Interchanging $\mathbf{X}$ and $\mathbf{Y}$  yields
$$
|\mathbf{Y}|-|\mathbf{X}|\le |\mathbf{Y}-\mathbf{X}|.
$$
Since $|\mathbf{X}-\mathbf{Y}|=|\mathbf{Y}-\mathbf{X}|$,
the last two inequalities imply the stated conclusion.
LLM_output:
```lean4
import Mathlib
import Aesop

set_option maxHeartbeats 0

open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat Filter

theorem norm_sub_ge_norm_sub_norm {n : ‚Ąē} (X Y : Fin n ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) :
    ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ ‚Č• |‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ - ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ| := by
  -- Let's denote the norm of a vector `v` as `‚ÄĖv‚ÄĖ`.
  -- We are given the triangle inequality: `‚ÄĖA + B‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖA‚ÄĖ + ‚ÄĖB‚ÄĖ`.

  -- The informal proof starts by rewriting X: X = Y + (X - Y).
  -- Then it applies the triangle inequality to this expression.
  have h1 : ‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ - ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ := by
    calc
      ‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ = ‚ÄĖY + (X - Y)‚ÄĖ := by
        rw [add_sub_cancel_left]
      _ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ + ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ := by
        -- This step uses the triangle inequality for vectors in ‚ĄĚ‚ĀŅ.
        -- The `norm_add_le` lemma from Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic provides this.
        apply norm_add_le
    linarith

  -- The informal proof then considers interchanging X and Y.
  -- This means we apply the same logic to Y = X + (Y - X).
  have h2 : ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ - ‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖY - X‚ÄĖ := by
    calc
      ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ = ‚ÄĖX + (Y - X)‚ÄĖ := by
        rw [add_sub_cancel_left]
      _ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ + ‚ÄĖY - X‚ÄĖ := by
        apply norm_add_le
    linarith

  -- Since ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ = ‚ÄĖY - X‚ÄĖ, we can substitute.
  rw [norm_sub_rev Y X] at h2

  -- Now we have two inequalities:
  -- 1. `‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ - ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ`
  -- 2. `‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ - ‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ`
  -- This second inequality is equivalent to `-(‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ - ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ) ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ`.
  -- These two together imply `|‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ - ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ| ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ`.
  exact abs_le.mpr ‚ü®h1, h2‚ü©
```

    ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ ‚Č• |‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ - ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ| := by
  -- Let's denote the norm of a vector `v` as `‚ÄĖv‚ÄĖ`.
  -- We are given the triangle inequality: `‚ÄĖA + B‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖA‚ÄĖ + ‚ÄĖB‚ÄĖ`.

  -- The informal proof starts by rewriting X: X = Y + (X - Y).
  -- Then it applies the triangle inequality to this expression.
  calc
    ‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ = ‚ÄĖY + (X - Y)‚ÄĖ := by
      rw [add_sub_cancel']
    _ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ + ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ := by
      -- This step uses the triangle inequality for vectors in ‚ĄĚ‚ĀŅ.
      -- The `norm_add_le` lemma from Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic provides this.
      apply norm_add_le

  -- From `‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ + ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ`, we deduce `‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ - ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ`.
  -- This is one part of the absolute value inequality.
  have h1 : ‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ - ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ := by
    linarith [norm_add_le X (X - Y)]

  -- The informal proof then considers interchanging X and Y.
  -- This means we apply the same logic to Y = X + (Y - X).
  calc
    ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ = ‚ÄĖX + (Y - X)‚ÄĖ := by
      rw [add_sub_cancel']
    _ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ + ‚ÄĖY - X‚ÄĖ := by
      apply norm_add_le

  -- We also know `‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ = ‚ÄĖ-(Y - X)‚ÄĖ = ‚ÄĖY - X‚ÄĖ` because `‚ÄĖ-v‚ÄĖ = ‚ÄĖv‚ÄĖ`.
  have h2 : ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ - ‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖY - X‚ÄĖ := by
    linarith [norm_add_le Y (Y - X)]

  -- Since ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ = ‚ÄĖY - X‚ÄĖ, we can substitute.
  rw [norm_sub_rev (Y) (X)] at h2

  -- Now we have two inequalities:
  -- 1. `‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ - ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ`
  -- 2. `‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ - ‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ`
  -- This second inequality is equivalent to `-(‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ - ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ) ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ`.
  -- These two together imply `|‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ - ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ| ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ`.
  exact abs_le.mpr ‚ü®h1, h2‚ü©
